question: What does a ReferenceError mean?

answer: You used a name that does not exist

answer: You did something a value cannot do for its type

question: What does a SyntaxError mean?

answer: JavaScript cannot parse the code so the file never runs

question: What is the golden rule when you see an error in the Console?

question: Why can the line number in a SyntaxError be misleading?

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: What does a breakpoint do?
options: Deletes a line of code|Pauses code execution at a specific line|Adds a console.log automatically|Fixes a bug in the code
answer: Pauses code execution at a specific line

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: Which DevTools tab do you use to set breakpoints?
options: Console|Elements|Sources|Network
answer: Sources

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: How do you set a breakpoint in the Sources tab?
options: Right-click the code and select Break|Click the line number|Type debugger in the Console|Press F5
answer: Click the line number

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: What appears on a line when you set a breakpoint?
options: A red circle|A blue marker|A yellow arrow|A green checkmark
answer: A blue marker

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: What does the Scope panel show while code is paused?
options: All HTML elements|Every variable's current value|The network requests|The CSS styles
answer: Every variable's current value

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: What does pressing F10 (Step Over) do when code is paused?
options: Resumes normal execution|Runs the current line and moves to the next|Enters a function call|Removes the breakpoint
answer: Runs the current line and moves to the next

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: What does pressing F11 (Step Into) do?

answer: Steps inside a function call on the current line

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: What does pressing F8 (Resume) do?
options: Steps to the next line|Enters a function call|Continues running until the next breakpoint|Closes DevTools
answer: Continues running until the next breakpoint

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: Which stepping control will you use 95% of the time?
options: F8 Resume|F10 Step Over|F11 Step Into|F5 Refresh
answer: F10 Step Over

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: What is the correct workflow after setting a breakpoint?
options: Close DevTools and reopen|Refresh the page so JavaScript pauses at the breakpoint|Click Run in the Console|Edit the HTML file
answer: Refresh the page so JavaScript pauses at the breakpoint

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: Why are breakpoints better than console.log for complex bugs?
options: They are faster to type|You can inspect every variable without adding and removing log statements|They fix bugs automatically|They work without DevTools
answer: You can inspect every variable without adding and removing log statements

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: Why would you set a breakpoint inside a loop?
options: To delete the loop|To watch how variables change on each iteration|To skip the loop entirely|To make the loop run faster
answer: To watch how variables change on each iteration

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: Values in the Scope panel update as you step forward through the code.
options: True|False
answer: True

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: When stepping through code with F10 how do you identify the bug?

answer: When a variable gets a wrong value in the Scope panel

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: If you set a breakpoint inside a loop and press F8 (Resume) what happens?
options: The loop stops|Code continues to the next breakpoint which is the same line on the next iteration|The page refreshes|The breakpoint is removed

title: breakpoints and stepping through code
question: When should you use Step Into (F11) instead of Step Over (F10)?
options: When you want to skip a line|When the current line calls a function and you want to see what happens inside it|When you want to resume normal execution|When you want to close DevTools
answer: When the current line calls a function and you want to see what happens inside it
